beijing.metaDescription: Complete guide to the Forbidden City, Great Wall, Temple of Heaven, hutongs, food, and transport.
beijing.intro: Beijing is China's capital with over 3,000 years of history. As an ancient imperial city, it hosts UNESCO sites including the Forbidden City, Temple of Heaven, and Summer Palace, plus the magnificent Badaling Great Wall. Hutong alleys preserve old Beijing life, while Guomao and Sanlitun showcase modern energy.
beijing.bestTime: Spring (Apr–May) and autumn (Sep–Oct) offer the most comfortable weather for the Great Wall and royal gardens. Winter brings snowy Forbidden City views—dress warmly.
beijing.transport: Capital and Daxing airports connect globally. The subway covers major sights; get a transit card. Reach the Great Wall via tourist bus or private car.

attractions.name: Forbidden City
attractions.desc: Ming and Qing imperial palace—the world's largest preserved wooden palace complex. Allow half to full day.

attractions.name: Badaling Great Wall
attractions.desc: The most iconic wall section, about 1.5 hours from downtown. Cable cars available.

attractions.name: Temple of Heaven
attractions.desc: Where emperors prayed for harvests. Hall of Prayer and Echo Wall are highlights—visit early morning.

attractions.name: Summer Palace
attractions.desc: China's largest royal garden—Kunming Lake and Longevity Hill make a perfect half-day trip.

foods.name: Peking Duck
foods.desc: Crispy skin, tender meat. Quanjude and Bianyifang are legendary—book ahead.

foods.name: Zhajiang Noodles
foods.desc: Beijing's classic noodle dish with fermented bean sauce.

foods.name: Douzhi & Jiaoquan
foods.desc: A unique traditional breakfast—bold flavors worth trying once.

shanghai.metaDescription: Guide to the Bund, Yu Garden, Lujiazui skyline, and Shanghai's Haipai culture.
shanghai.intro: Shanghai is China's largest economic hub and global metropolis. Colonial architecture on the Bund faces Lujiazui's skyscrapers across the Huangpu River. Shikumen lanes and Xintiandi preserve Haipai culture; Nanjing Road and Huaihai Road are shopping paradises.
shanghai.bestTime: Spring (Mar–May) and autumn (Sep–Nov) are ideal. Summers are hot and humid; winters are damp but festive.
shanghai.transport: Pudong and Hongqiao airports. Extensive metro network; maglev train to Pudong is an experience. Walk or ferry between Bund and Lujiazui.

attractions.name: The Bund
attractions.desc: Iconic waterfront with colonial buildings—best at dusk and night.

attractions.name: Yu Garden
attractions.desc: Ming-era private garden plus City God Temple food street—try xiaolongbao here.

attractions.name: Lujiazui
attractions.desc: Oriental Pearl Tower and Shanghai Tower observation decks offer stunning views.

attractions.name: Tianzifang
attractions.desc: Shikumen lanes turned creative district with cafés, shops, and galleries.

foods.name: Xiaolongbao
foods.desc: Soup dumplings—Nanxiang and Din Tai Fung are favorites.

foods.name: Shengjianbao
foods.desc: Pan-fried buns with crispy bottoms and juicy filling.

foods.name: Benbang Cuisine
foods.desc: Red-braised pork and oil-blasted shrimp—rich local flavors.

xian.metaDescription: Terracotta Army, city wall, Giant Wild Goose Pagoda, and Muslim Quarter food guide.
xian.intro: Xi'an, ancient Chang'an, was capital of thirteen dynasties and the Silk Road's eastern terminus. Its city wall is China's best preserved. The Terracotta Army is the 'Eighth Wonder of the World.' Muslim Quarter food and Tang-era landmarks tell two millennia of history.
xian.bestTime: Spring (Mar–May) and autumn (Sep–Nov) are best. Summers are hot; winters are cold but less crowded.
xian.transport: Xianyang Airport is ~40 km from downtown. Metro covers main sights; walk or bike inside the wall. Tourist buses to the Terracotta Army.

attractions.name: Terracotta Army
attractions.desc: Vast underground army museum—hire a guide or audio tour; allow 3–4 hours.

attractions.name: Xi'an City Wall
attractions.desc: China's most complete ancient wall—cycle or walk the perimeter; South Gate at night is beautiful.

attractions.name: Giant Wild Goose Pagoda
attractions.desc: Tang-era pagoda where Xuanzang translated sutras; north square fountain show is popular.

attractions.name: Muslim Quarter
attractions.desc: Food street paradise—yangrou paomo, roujiamo, and local sweets are must-tries.

foods.name: Yangrou Paomo
foods.desc: Lamb soup with hand-torn bread—Lao Sunjia and Tongshengxiang are classics.

foods.name: Roujiamo
foods.desc: Chinese 'burger' with braised pork in crispy flatbread.

foods.name: Biangbiang Noodles
foods.desc: Wide belt-like noodles with bold, spicy sauce.

chengdu.metaDescription: Giant pandas, Kuanzhai Alley, Dujiangyan, and Sichuan cuisine guide.
chengdu.intro: Chengdu, heart of the Land of Abundance, is famed for relaxed living, spicy food, and giant pandas. Kuanzhai Alley and Jinli preserve Sichuan architecture; Dujiangyan and Mount Qingcheng showcase ancient engineering. Tea houses, mahjong, and face-changing opera are local experiences.
chengdu.bestTime: Spring (Mar–Jun) and autumn (Sep–Nov) are comfortable. Summers are humid; winters are cloudy but heated indoors.
chengdu.transport: Shuangliu and Tianfu airports. Metro is convenient; visit the panda base early. High-speed rail to Dujiangyan in ~30 minutes.

attractions.name: Giant Panda Base
attractions.desc: Visit early morning for feeding time; arrive before 8 AM and use shuttle buses inside.

attractions.name: Kuanzhai Alley
attractions.desc: Qing-era streets with teahouses, snacks, and creative shops.

attractions.name: Dujiangyan
attractions.desc: 2,200-year-old irrigation system by Li Bing—UNESCO site, pair with Mount Qingcheng.

attractions.name: Jinli Ancient Street
attractions.desc: Replica old street with great night atmosphere, snacks, and Sichuan opera.

foods.name: Hot Pot
foods.desc: Spicy and numbing—order yuanyang (half-half) if you're new to mala.

foods.name: Dan Dan Noodles
foods.desc: Sesame-rich noodles with chili oil and minced pork.

foods.name: Fuqi Feipian
foods.desc: Classic cold dish of sliced beef and offal in chili oil.

guilin.metaDescription: Li River cruise, Yangshuo, Longji Rice Terraces, and karst landscape guide.
guilin.intro: Guilin's karst peaks along the Li River create China's most iconic landscapes. A river cruise to Yangshuo feels like floating through a painting. Longji Terraces glow golden in autumn; Elephant Trunk Hill and Two Rivers Four Lakes are city landmarks.
guilin.bestTime: Apr–Oct is ideal; Sep–Oct for golden terraces. Spring mist on the Li River is poetic.
guilin.transport: Guilin Liangjiang Airport. Buses or cruises to Yangshuo; charter or day tours to Longji Terraces.

attractions.name: Li River Cruise
attractions.desc: Yangshuo section is most scenic—4–5 hours; book bamboo raft or cruise ahead.

attractions.name: Yangshuo West Street
attractions.desc: Blend of East and West with bars, restaurants, and guesthouses—lively at night.

attractions.name: Longji Rice Terraces
attractions.desc: Zhuang and Yao villages amid terraces—Jinkeng Dazhai has the best views; stay overnight.

attractions.name: Elephant Trunk Hill
attractions.desc: Guilin's symbol—a hill shaped like an elephant drinking from the river.

foods.name: Guilin Rice Noodles
foods.desc: Slippery noodles in savory broth—local favorites abound.

foods.name: Beer Fish
foods.desc: Yangshuo specialty—fresh Li River fish cooked with beer.

foods.name: Lipu Taro Pork
foods.desc: Layers of taro and pork belly—soft, fragrant banquet dish.

hangzhou.metaDescription: West Lake, Lingyin Temple, Longjing tea, and Hangzhou cultural guide.
hangzhou.intro: Hangzhou, praised as 'Paradise on Earth,' is defined by West Lake's Ten Scenes. Su Causeway, Broken Bridge, and Three Pools Mirroring the Moon each have unique charm. Lingyin Temple draws pilgrims; Longjing Village offers tea culture; Hefang Street recalls the Southern Song capital.
hangzhou.bestTime: Spring (Mar–May) for blossoms, autumn (Sep–Nov) for osmanthus. Summer lotus blooms; winter snow on Broken Bridge is iconic.
hangzhou.transport: Hangzhou Xiaoshan Airport. Metro reaches West Lake; cycle or boat around the lake; buses to Lingyin Temple.

attractions.name: West Lake
attractions.desc: Cycle the lake, boat to Three Pools, climb Leifeng Pagoda for panoramas.

attractions.name: Lingyin Temple
attractions.desc: Ancient temple with Feilai Peak carvings—combine with Yongfu and Taoguang temples.

attractions.name: Longjing Village
attractions.desc: Taste pre-Qingming tea, pick leaves, hike Nine Creeks through misty hills.

attractions.name: Hefang Street
attractions.desc: Song Dynasty street with time-honored shops like Huqingyutang pharmacy.

foods.name: West Lake Vinegar Fish
foods.desc: Sweet-and-sour grass fish—Lou Wai Lou is the classic restaurant.

foods.name: Dongpo Pork
foods.desc: Slow-braised pork belly—tender, rich, linked to poet Su Dongpo.

foods.name: Longjing Shrimp
foods.desc: Shrimp stir-fried with Longjing tea leaves—signature Hangzhou dish.
